name: Core
summary: HealthOS law-bearing contracts for identity, habilitation, consent, finalidade, gate, provenance, storage law, and lawfulContext.
invariants: HealthOS Core remains sovereign for consent, habilitation, finality, gate, provenance, storage law, and lawfulContext.
AACI, GOS, apps, providers, and construction tooling consume Core-mediated surfaces and never become Core law.
Drafts do not become final artifacts without approved gate resolution.
Scaffold maturity must never be described as production readiness.
allowedWork: Maintain Core governance contracts and fail-closed validators.
Update Swift, TypeScript, JSON Schema, and SQL mirrors together when a Core contract changes.
Add or repair tests that prove Core boundary behavior.
Clarify official docs when Core sovereignty or maturity wording drifts.
forbiddenWork: Move Core law into apps, AACI, GOS, providers, or construction tooling.
Weaken consent, habilitation, gate, finality, provenance, or lawfulContext checks.
Treat app UI, GOS policy, or provider output as clinical authority.
Claim production readiness, full EHR status, or real regulatory/provider integration.
knownGaps: Production clinical, regulatory, provider, and interoperability integrations remain out of scope.
Some Core contracts are scaffolded or tested operational paths rather than production-hardened systems.
notes: Territory records are construction metadata only; official docs remain canonical.
Any Core change has high blast radius and should include contract and validation updates.
